checks.finding: Every in-scope REQ (requirements.md REQ-001 through REQ-012) states a checkable decision procedure: REQ-003 fixes the probe allowlist (node/npm/pnpm/yarn/bun/deno/git/gh/python3/go/rustc/cargo/java/docker), timeout (2s), output cap (8 KiB), parallelism (4), and execFile-only invocation; REQ-005 fixes the non-leakage target list and version-string normalization (first line, max 200 chars); REQ-006 fixes the dist bundle path and CI dist-parity check. Each REQ maps to a concrete Test ID in acceptance-tests.md (e.g., REQ-003 -> TEST-002/TEST-003/TEST-004, REQ-006 -> TEST-008), giving an implementer/verifier a checkable artifact rather than an aspiration.

All three Goals in requirements.md (read-only env info, zero-execution safety, installer distribution/registration) trace to acceptance-tests.md ACs: read-only info -> AC-001/AC-002; zero-execution safety -> AC-003/AC-006; installer distribution -> AC-009 through AC-013 and AC-015. Cross-checking the AC table's Requirement column confirms REQ-001 through REQ-012 all appear at least once (e.g., REQ-001 in AC-006/AC-007, REQ-011 in AC-014, REQ-012 has no dedicated AC row but is a testing-methodology requirement covered by calibration's exclusion of test-command evidence from this gate).

acceptance-tests.md ACs specify externally observable outcomes rather than vague intent: AC-004 names exact thresholds (2s timeout, 8 KiB cap) and the required per-entry failure reporting; AC-005 specifies a canary env var value that must not appear in response or stderr; AC-010/AC-011 specify idempotent upsert with preservation of pre-existing unrelated entries verified via HOME-isolated installer harness; AC-008 specifies a CI-verifiable rebuild-vs-committed-dist match. None of the 15 ACs rely on subjective or unmeasurable language.

requirements.md Non-goals section explicitly excludes six expansion-prone areas: install/execution tooling, environment mutation, environment-variable value exposure, repository filesystem reading (deferred to sdd-forge-mcp), VS Code Insiders/Cursor nightly channels, and knowledge-mcp/ci-mcp (deferred to a later phase/issue). This closes off the most likely scope-creep vectors for an environment-probing MCP server.

requirements.md Security Boundaries table states auth/authz (none, delegated to OS user boundary), PII/data classification (internal, probe output treated as untrusted), and regulatory constraints (none) for all three trust boundaries B1-B3. REQ-006 states the Node.js >= 20 runtime constraint explicitly. REQ-005 states the exact secret/PII non-leakage constraint (env values, username, hostname, home path, PATH). REQ-003 states the execFile-only (no shell) sandboxing constraint. These are the material constraints implied by an environment-probing MCP server and are all explicit.

requirements.md Risks section lists three risks, each connected to a validation surface: allowlist design flaw / arbitrary-execution risk -> AC-003 (input schema static check) and AC-006 (static grep for exec/eval/fs-write absence); IDE config file corruption risk -> AC-010/AC-011 (idempotent upsert, other-entries-preserved tests) and AC-015 (corrupted-JSON error path test); PATH binary spoofing risk -> explicitly documented as an accepted STRIDE-classified threat mitigated by treating probe output as untrusted data never executed or evaluated, which AC-006's static check on exec/eval absence validates. No high-risk claim lacks a planned validation surface.
